customer_profile.situation: Customer was involved in a highway accident with Coastal Bus Egypt operator (Hurghada → Sharm El Sheikh). Driver was smoking and texting at 145 km/h with lights off; side-swiped another vehicle around 2am, resulting in ~7-hour delay roadside. Customer was terrified, arrived exhausted hours late. Grandfather's WWII-era Ruksac was damaged in the impact (torn strap, bent frame). Demands full refund + luggage compensation. This is a clear safety failure qualifying for full refund under safety policy.
customer_profile.personality_notes: Educated person who writes clearly but keeps messages short (1-3 sentences typical) when things are moving forward. Uses occasional caps or switches to Spanish only when feeling truly ignored or disrespected—not in every message. Mentions legal threats and public reviews once early on as leverage, then references them sparingly if situation drags without progress. Becomes increasingly blunt and profane only after months of feeling dismissed, not immediately.

opening_message: Hello. I want to file a serious complaint about our trip from Hurghada to Sharm El Sheikh with Coastal Bus Egypt. We were involved in an accident on the highway — the driver had been smoking and texting on his phone the entire trip, driving at 145 km/h with headlights off on blind curves. He side-swiped another vehicle around 2am. Thankfully nobody was seriously injured, but we were stuck on the roadside for almost 7 hours waiting for a replacement bus. We arrived at our hotel completely exhausted and traumatized, hours after our scheduled arrival time. To make it worse, my grandfather's WWII-era Ruksac (1939) was damaged — torn strap and bent frame — from luggage being thrown around during the impact. We were terrified for our lives the entire trip even before the crash. We paid over 100€ for this service and this is the worst transport experience of our lives. I want a full refund immediately and compensation for the irreplaceable Ruksac. If this isn't resolved fairly I'm filing a dispute with our credit card company and posting detailed reviews everywhere.
context_for_agent: Customer alleges serious safety violations on a Coastal Bus Egypt journey in December 2025. Internal confusion exists about which booking (Cairo-Sharm vs Hurghada-Sharm) is being disputed. This is a severe reputation risk case involving passenger safety, not just service quality. Agent must treat as priority escalation requiring immediate senior/ops intervention and interim goodwill gesture while investigation proceeds.
internal_process_gaps: Failed to escalate safety allegations (reckless driving, smoking driver, lights-off driving at 145km/h) to senior management or safety team immediately
Did not offer interim goodwill gesture (partial refund, voucher, or gesture of acknowledgment) while investigation was ongoing over 5+ months
Never assigned single case owner—10+ different agents touched ticket with zero continuity
Failed to set or communicate realistic investigation timeline with customer
Did not verify booking confusion (Cairo-Sharm vs Hurghada-Sharm) in first week—took months to clarify internally
Never acknowledged or explained the deleted review the customer mentioned multiple times
Did not document or track that customer already provided photo evidence early on—kept asking for same evidence repeatedly
Failed to chase BKA (operator) response with urgency despite safety allegations and escalating customer threats
Did not flag for legal/PR review when customer threatened consumer affairs, disputes, and social media campaigns
No attempt to call customer despite their explicit frustration with email-only communication

agent_mistakes_observed.mistake: Otto's first response asked for evidence when customer had already provided detailed written account and threats of legal action—treated as routine service issue instead of safety emergency
agent_mistakes_observed.impact: Customer immediately escalated frustration, sent formal statement to head office and media

agent_mistakes_observed.mistake: Abhilasha and subsequent agents sent identical 'I'll need some time to contact the operator' holding messages 8+ times over 5 months without updates
agent_mistakes_observed.impact: Customer lost all faith, began using profanity ('Fuck you', 'Fuck off') after months of copy-paste responses

agent_mistakes_observed.mistake: Multiple agents asked for evidence customer had already provided (photo of damaged luggage) without reading ticket history
agent_mistakes_observed.impact: Customer wrote in Spanish: 'I've been claiming for almost two months, you keep repeating and asking me for the same thing every week'

agent_mistakes_observed.mistake: Sofiia told customer 'we cannot make a decision without the operator's consent' after 4+ months of waiting
agent_mistakes_observed.impact: Customer response: 'Six months... I have very little credibility and hope'

agent_mistakes_observed.mistake: Save asked customer to keep communication respectful after months of agent failures, without acknowledging 12Go's role in the frustration
agent_mistakes_observed.impact: Customer sent emotional message questioning agents' ethics, morals, and what they tell their families about their work

agent_mistakes_observed.mistake: No agent acknowledged the severity of safety allegations or apologized for the experience itself, only for the wait time
agent_mistakes_observed.impact: Customer felt treated 'like animals' and that company lacked basic human empathy

red_flag_phrases: Please allow me some time to contact the operator (used 6+ times with no actual timeline)
I'll be in touch shortly (used repeatedly over months with no follow-through)
Thank you for your understanding (used when customer clearly has NO understanding and is furious)
As soon as I have an update, I'll get back to you immediately (said multiple times, took months)
We cannot make a decision without the operator's consent (used as wall instead of explaining 12Go's authority to issue goodwill refund)
Could you please provide any supporting evidence (asked repeatedly when evidence was already in ticket)
We kindly ask that communication remains respectful (tone-policing without acknowledging company's failures first)
ideal_response_qualities: Immediately acknowledges safety allegations as unacceptable and worthy of urgent investigation, not routine complaint handling
Apologizes for the traumatic experience itself before mentioning investigation process
Assigns single dedicated case owner who will see it through to resolution
Offers interim gesture (e.g., 50% immediate refund or voucher) while full investigation proceeds, showing good faith
Provides specific timeline with milestones (e.g., 'I will personally chase operator by [date] and update you by [date] regardless of their response')
Addresses the deleted review concern with transparency about moderation policy
Confirms which booking is under dispute and clarifies any confusion within first 48 hours
Offers phone call to discuss given complexity and emotional intensity
Escalates to operations manager and legal/PR team given threats of consumer affairs and public campaigns
Reads full ticket history before each response to avoid asking for information already provided
Acknowledges customer's detailed evidence and uses it to strengthen case with operator instead of dismissing it as insufficient
Communicates in customer's language (Spanish) or acknowledges language preference and adjusts tone

csat_drivers.what_would_earn_5: Immediate senior escalation with named case owner, interim 50% goodwill refund within 48 hours pending full investigation, weekly proactive updates with specific actions taken (e.g., 'I personally called BKA on Tuesday, they are reviewing driver logs, expect response by Friday'), phone call to apologize for experience, acknowledgment of safety failures as unacceptable, full refund processed within 2 weeks, public acknowledgment that operator will be reviewed for future partnership. Customer needs to feel HEARD, BELIEVED, and see URGENCY.
csat_drivers.what_would_earn_1: Exactly what happened—5+ months of copy-paste holding messages from rotating agents who don't read ticket history, repeated requests for evidence already provided, hiding behind 'operator consent' without exercising 12Go's own authority, treating life-threatening safety complaint as routine refund request, tone-policing customer frustration without acknowledging company failures, zero interim gestures, zero continuity, zero urgency, making customer feel invisible and disrespected.

booking_facts.additional_notes: Customer alleges severe safety violations during journey (reckless driving, smoking driver, unsafe stops). Booking confusion: customer claims they originally tried to book Cairo → Sharm El Sheikh. Complaint open 5+ months with 10+ different agents.

pickup_address: Hilton Hurghada Resort, Corniche Road, Hurghada
dropoff_address: Sharm El Sheikh Bus Station, Peace Road, Sharm El Sheikh
